title: Change un/pwd handling to match Buildah's
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Changes the un/pwd handling to match Buildah's.  The username, password or a blank can be passed to the creds param.  The user will be prompted for the unsupplied values.  The username will be echo'd, the password will not.  

Removes the password handling from the pull and push code into the common code.

comments.body: LGTM, though I'd like to see a bit of code moved before merge
Thanks @mheon, will move and push a new commit.  I'm not quite sure what to make of the build errors, looks like a network issue.  Will dive deeper if they re-occur.
I take that back, tests found an error in my code.  Will touch that up too.
LGTM

@TomSweeneyRedHat Could you just verify that this fails when you don't have a terminal?

podman pull --creds ... < /dev/null

@rhatdan It fails  with < /dev/null unless the appropriate username and password is supplied.  The case where the password is supplied but the username is not is a bit messy, but I doubt anyone would ever use that.  Each command returns to the command prompt without user interaction. 

Holler if I messed up the test.

```
# podman pull --cert-dir /root/auth --creds "testuser" localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom < /dev/null
Password: could not read password from terminal: inappropriate ioctl for device
# podman pull --cert-dir /root/auth --creds "" localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom < /dev/null
Username: Password: could not read password from terminal: inappropriate ioctl for device
# podman pull --cert-dir /root/auth --creds ":testpassword" localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom < /dev/null
Username: Trying to pull localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom:latest...
Failed
error pulling image "localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom": error pulling image from "localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom:latest: unauthorized: authentication required
# podman pull --cert-dir /root/auth --creds "testuser:testpassword" localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom < /dev/null
Trying to pull localhost:5000/my-alpine-tom:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:ff3a5c916c92643ff77519ffa742d3ec61b7f591b6b7504599d95a4a41134e28
Copying config sha256:3fd9065eaf02feaf94d68376da52541925650b81698c53c6824d92ff63f98353
 1.48 KB / 1.48 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

Ok I just wanted to make sure it did not hang.
LGTM
@TomSweeneyRedHat I like @mheon idea of moving this to a pkg subdir, then we could vendor it into buildah and share the code between the two packages.

@rhatdan I believe I moved the files to the location that @mheon requested.  @mheon, please correct me if I'm off base.  I'm happy to move this to a more central location for Buildah/Podman use, but think that should be part of a larger effort to get the two playing nicer together and would like to get this merged in the short term.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Yeah, util.go is a fine place for it to land I think. If we want to move it further into a vendorable subpackage we can do that too (`pkg/util/`?)
Put it in pkg/util. And then we can merge.
bot, retest this please
All green lights.  @rhatdan I think I tucked the code where you wanted it.  Holler if not.
@rh-atomic-bot r+
